narration: There's probably a gift card buried in your home right now — and retailers deliberately designed every part of it to stay unspent.
visual: Dark background. A drawer SVG springs up from below containing three colorful gift cards ($50 purple, $25 red, $100 teal) tilted at various angles inside. '$175 IN DEAD MONEY' text springs in large below the drawer. Subtitle 'AND RETAILERS PLANNED IT THAT WAY' fades in underneath.

narration: Gift cards have expiration fees, narrow merchant restrictions, and awkward checkout fields — every single one of those obstacles was deliberately built into the design.
visual: Light background. Header 'EVERY OBSTACLE WAS DESIGNED ON PURPOSE' fades in. Three colored panels spring up one by one: red panel with clock-X icon (EXPIRATION FEES), amber panel with padlock icon (LIMITED MERCHANTS), purple panel with error-form icon (CHECKOUT FRICTION). Each has a short description below the icon.

narration: Retailers have an actual accounting term for the money you forget. It's called 'breakage' — and it generates over a billion dollars in annual profit.
visual: Dark background. Large 'BREAKAGE' text scales in dramatically in green. Subtitle 'their word for your forgotten money' fades in. Person silhouette on left, store building on right, three dollar signs animate from the person across to the store building. '$1 BILLION / YEAR IN PURE PROFIT' appears below.

narration: Here's the trap: your brain values a gift card more than cash of the same amount — but you'll actually spend it 40% less.
visual: Light background. 'THE BRAIN GLITCH' header. Two panels slide in from opposite sides: left green panel shows happy face emoji holding a gift card labeled 'FEELS BETTER'; right red panel shows confused face with forgotten gray card labeled 'SPENT 40% LESS'. 'VS' appears between them.

narration: Americans are holding twenty-one billion dollars in unspent gift card value right now. The average household has $175 just sitting there, doing nothing.
visual: Dark background. 'UNSPENT GIFT CARDS IN AMERICA' label. Animated counter ticks from $0 to $21 with 'BILLION' label beside it in white. Green progress bar fills across the screen beneath. Two stat boxes fade in below: '$175 per household sitting idle' and '$3B expires unused every year' in red.

narration: Check your drawer, your wallet, your old emails — you've probably got real money sitting there. Spend it or sell it this week.
visual: Light background. 'DON'T LET THEM WIN' header. Phone SVG showing gift card balance of $175.00 springs in on the left. Three numbered action steps fade in on the right: 1) Search your drawer, wallet, and old emails 2) Use them at checkout before buying with cash 3) Sell unwanted cards — get up to 92 cents on the dollar. Green 'FOLLOW FOR MORE MONEY PSYCHOLOGY' CTA fades in at bottom.
